summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering trick-or-treat planning, ARP funding updates, zoning nuisance and junk vehicle resolutions for two properties, budget appropriation increases, and community updates. Significant time was spent on declaring nuisances and junk motor vehicles at 6833 Linton Road (three parcels with 12 junk vehicles) and 6952 Goshen Road. Budget increases were approved for cemetery mowing, firefighter turnout gear, and fire/EMS fuel costs. Community updates included the sale of the Green property, new businesses, and upcoming Light Up Goshen Parade planning.

Harry Holbrook to conduct lien holder searches and bring in contractors for nuisance property cleanup at 6833 Linton Road and 6952 Goshen Road

Staff to post trick-or-treat and trunk-or-treat information on Facebook and front sign
Bob Hawes/Chamber to submit formal road use request for Light Up Goshen Parade for next meeting agenda
Steve Pegram to email department heads to check fuel budget numbers for potential shortfalls
Staff to begin posting monthly department activity report PDFs on township website

Park Board meeting tomorrow to develop list of future action items
key_amounts: $199.98 for civilian tire repair from spike strip incident
$3,700 increase in cemetery fund for additional mowing (3 mows)
$18,000 increase in fire/EMS fund for turnout gear replacement (20 sets at ~$2,500 each)
$6,500 increase in fire/EMS fund for fuel
$25,000 annual budget for 10 sets of turnout gear
~$900,000 ARP funds received (first half), second payment expected next year
$8,300 zoning revenue collected in September, within $300 of 2021 projections
~$20,000 received from surplus fire/EMS equipment sales
$7.5 billion proposed federal fire station construction grant program
notable_topics: Trick-or-treat and trunk-or-treat planning for October 31
ARP funding update - still under temporary rules, limited use for townships
Village of Belmont final development plan - special hearing scheduled November 3
Nuisance property declarations and junk vehicle resolutions at 6833 Linton Road and 6952 Goshen Road
Budget appropriation increases for cemetery mowing, turnout gear, and fuel
Congressional Fire Caucus update on potential fire station construction grants
Goshen Chamber of Commerce Light Up Goshen Parade request for November 20
Green property sale and Ben Puckett building sale to Critter Gone LLC
Police department training new pre-due officers
Multiple grant applications submitted for road projects (sidewalks, paving, turn lanes)
Chamber shifting focus from community events to business advocacy
notes: The meeting featured an unusually large number of individual resolutions (12 junk vehicles and 4 nuisance declarations) for properties at 6833 Linton Road across three parcels. Harry Holbrook noted these properties are becoming like the notorious 'Donnie Combs' situation. A special meeting was scheduled for November 3 at 5:30 PM to hear the Village of Belmont final development plan case (Zoning Commission case 2021-05) due to statutory timeline requirements. The video title says 10.11.21 but the meeting was held on October 12, 2021. A police pursuit of a murder suspect on October 1st resulted in a civilian's tire being damaged by spike strips.
title: BOT 10.11.21.mp4
